summary: Identified all field-name mismatches between Lean dataset_generator JSONL output and Python schema layers; sync workflow via make sync-data is ready; ingestion.py adapter is the critical missing implementation.

artifacts.summary: Complete gap analysis of Lean export fields vs Python schema (records.py and schema.py), with mapping table, prioritized fix list, and sync workflow specification.

memory_candidates.content: BimodalLogic lake exe dataset_generator emits JSONL with camelCase PatternKey keys (modalDepth, temporalDepth, impCount, topOperator), lowercase labels (valid/invalid/timeout), ProofTrace as {height, axioms_used: string[], rules_applied: string[]}, SimpleCountermodel.trueAtoms as Atom objects {base, fresh_index}, and DifficultyMetrics in camelCase (decisionTimeMs, difficultyTier as string). The canonical Python consumer is TrainingRecord in schema/records.py; schema.py LabeledFormula is a secondary layer with several wrong field mappings.

BimodalHarness sync workflow: (1) In BimodalLogic run 'lake exe dataset_generator -- --max-complexity 5 --output data/bmlogic.jsonl'. (2) In BimodalHarness run 'make sync-data BIMODAL_LOGIC_PATH=../BimodalLogic' which rsyncs *.jsonl and *.parquet to data/bimodal/. (3) Run 'make validate-data'. (4) Update data/VERSION with the BimodalLogic git commit. The bimodal/ subdirectory is gitignored; samples/ is tracked.
